title: The Moonlit Apothecary
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: In a border town famous for healing waters, apothecary Elian Voss inherits a locked moon garden and a ledger of night-blooming plants.
long_description:
In a border town famous for healing waters, apothecary Elian Voss inherits a locked moon garden and a ledger of night-blooming plants. When patients recover from impossible ailments and then die in precise lunar intervals, Elian joins a skeptical physician and a disgraced constable to trace a poison through family debt, civic pride, and a charitable hospital built over an older crime.

The novel is a spare, dialogue-driven investigation built around adult characters, concrete work, and evidence that changes meaning under pressure. It follows its central investigator through fifty chapters of discovery, argument, pursuit, and public proof, with each chapter turning on a material clue rather than exposition.

The story emphasizes the ethics of records: who gets named, who gets erased, and what a careful person owes to the dead when institutions prefer a clean account.
main_characters: Elian Voss - a careful apothecary who trusts plants more than public men
Dr. Mara Celyn - a practical physician whose clinic receives the first impossible cures
Inspector Rowan Vale - a dismissed constable trying to prove his last case was not madness
Sister Adela Pryce - the hospital matron who knows which records were burned
Ivor March - a hotel owner whose fortune depends on the town's reputation for health
